preamble: The shift from the voiceless velar fricative [x] to the voiceless glottal fricative [h] is a classic example of debuccalization (lenition). This process involves the loss of an oral place of articulation, moving the friction from the soft palate to the glottis.

examples.note: The standard velar fricative /x/ (jota) debuccalizes to [h] in many coastal and southern dialects.
Historical [x] (written 'gh') first debuccalized to [h] before disappearing entirely in most positions.

sources: Penny, R. (2002). A History of the Spanish Language.
Lass, R. (1997). Historical Linguistics and Language Change.
